business_rules.text: Hooks (stop.py, post_tool_use.py) and sidebar.py are already POSIX-compatible; /tmp directory usage requires no platform-specific changes
business_rules.evidence: The rest of the codebase (hooks, sidebar.py) is already POSIX-compatible — `/tmp` works fine on Linux so no need to touch those.

business_rules.text: Terminal launcher must detect platform and use appropriate terminal emulator; must include fallback for unknown systems
business_rules.evidence: Create `launch_sidebar.sh` (Linux equivalent of `launch_sidebar.command`)... Update `start_sidebar.sh` to add Linux terminal detection between the macOS blocks and the fallback

business_rules.text: Plugin loads stateless at session startup via --plugin-dir flag, registering skills and hooks available to all subsequent interactions
business_rules.evidence: claude --plugin-dir /mnt/work/anchor registers skills like /anchor:seed and /anchor:companion; The plugin is stateless at startup

business_rules.text: Seed operation must run once per project to bootstrap spec.json files; subsequent sessions load and monitor existing specs
business_rules.evidence: Seed it once... it'll ask you a few setup questions... then spin up parallel agents... Each session after that, just run /anchor:companion... That loads the relevant specs into context

tradeoffs.decision: Manual skill execution via SKILL.md reading instead of automatic plugin-driven execution in current session
tradeoffs.reason: Plugin was not pre-loaded in current session; manual execution achieves identical functional result
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Requires explicit instruction-following; loses automatic context integration and plugin hooks
tradeoffs.evidence: The plugin isn't loaded in this session, but I can read the SKILL.md and run it manually — same effect.

tradeoffs.decision: Platform-specific terminal detection logic with fallback case in start_sidebar.sh
tradeoffs.reason: Different terminal emulators available on different OSes (Terminal.app on macOS, gnome-terminal on Linux, etc.)
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Increases code complexity and ongoing maintenance burden across platforms
tradeoffs.evidence: Create `launch_sidebar.sh` (Linux equivalent of `launch_sidebar.command`)... Update `start_sidebar.sh` to add Linux terminal detection between the macOS blocks and the fallback

decisions.text: Create Linux support for Anchor plugin and contribute changes back via PR to original public repository
decisions.evidence: Let's see if we can get it working in Linux, and if we do, I'll PR his repo and give it to him.

decisions.text: Create launch_sidebar.sh as Linux equivalent of macOS launch_sidebar.command file
Add Linux terminal detection to start_sidebar.sh before fallback case
decisions.evidence: Update `start_sidebar.sh` to add Linux terminal detection between the macOS blocks and the fallback

decisions.text: Update README to document Linux support
decisions.evidence: Now update the README to reflect Linux support

decisions.text: Install uv as prerequisite for sidebar functionality
decisions.evidence: `uv` isn't installed. That's a prerequisite... Want me to install it? USER: yes, please do

decisions.text: Use gnome-terminal for Linux sidebar launching
decisions.evidence: `gnome-terminal` is available and `$DISPLAY=:0` — we can test it.

decisions.text: Push changes to private fork and open PR against original public repository
decisions.evidence: commit our changes, push to your fork, then open the PR targeting his repo

decisions.text: Install GitHub CLI (gh) for command-line PR operations
decisions.evidence: lets install it, I'll probably need it again... gh auth login to authenticate with SSH/GPG setup

decisions.text: Plugin flag can be combined with session resume flag: claude -c --plugin-dir
decisions.evidence: You can combine the flags: cd /mnt/work/cora && claude -c --plugin-dir /mnt/work/anchor... That resumes your last cora session with Anchor loaded.

session_purpose: Add Linux support to Anchor plugin, test the implementation, open PR to original repo, and plan integration approach for TypeScript monorepo project.
